signals.reasoning: Keep managing the live MON short. It is still retaining about 79% of peak P&L, has no active warning pressure, and continues to behave like a real winner.
BTC is steady, but the active veto keeps benchmark exposure in watch mode rather than action mode.
ETH is too mixed to justify a fresh trade while the event brake is active.
SOL remains a valid long candidate on mechanics, but veto keeps it in watch mode for now.
ZEREBRO still lacks the trade quality and clean edge needed for action.
STBL remains a clean bearish fade because funding is strongly positive, crowding is high, and the market still looks vulnerable to reversal.
STABLE has some constructive bias, but not enough quality to justify a trade under veto conditions.
BLUR has improving squeeze-long characteristics, but the event brake keeps it in watch mode rather than buy mode.
DASH remains one of the best long candidates on pure mechanics, but veto should keep it sidelined for now.
CHILLGUY remains extended and crowded, but not clean enough to trade during the veto window.
